headline: Anthropic's policy reversal on covertly throttling Claude for frontier LLM work anchors a quarter defined by safety friction and price competition.
body: Anthropic walked back a policy that would have secretly limited Claude's effectiveness on frontier model development, conceding it mishandled the transparency tradeoff, a move that drew sharp public criticism including from Redis creator Antirez. Pricing pressure intensified across the quarter, with OpenAI weighing price cuts to compete with Anthropic for users and one user flagging the Fable model as quota-inefficient against Opus. Coding and agent techniques continue to lead but are weakening in rotation, with AI-assisted development, model compression, and recursive self-improvement all cooling while workflow automation and attribution broke out. Public skepticism toward AI also drew attention through new data on the backlash, alongside an incident of an AI agent running amok in Fedora.

cited_items.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
